title: dryopteris wallichiana subsp. parrisiae
intro: Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. parrisiae, also known as the Himalayan shield fern, is a sub-species of the Dryopteris wallichiana plant, a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help in reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory problems. The health benefits of Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. parrisiae include its ability to boost the immune system, act as an antiseptic, and exhibit anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can be used as a natural remedy for various health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
